impressive_projects.title: PDF Document Processor
impressive_projects.desc: Build a PDF parser with text extraction, annotation support, form filling, and digital signatures.

impressive_projects.title: Image Editing Web App
impressive_projects.desc: Browser-based image editor with layers, filters, crop/resize, and undo/redo stack.

impressive_projects.title: Creative Asset Manager
impressive_projects.desc: DAM system with tagging, version control, preview generation, and team collaboration.

must_have: Creative technology or design tools projects stand out

interview_reviews.question: 2 coding + aptitude questions
interview_reviews.quote: HackerRank OA. Mix of DSA coding and output-based questions. Arrays/strings focus. 90 min.

interview_reviews.question: Recursion + Graph DFS with memoization
interview_reviews.quote: Deep DSA focus. They ask about recursive solutions, then optimize with DP. Theory + implementation.

interview_reviews.question: LLD: Design Facebook comments system
interview_reviews.quote: Class design, data members, methods. OOP evaluation. They want to see SOLID principles.

interview_reviews.question: OS concepts (virtual memory, paging) + pointer questions in C++
interview_reviews.quote: CS fundamentals round. OS, DBMS, and C++ deep questions. Memory management is key.

interview_reviews.question: Behavioral + project deep-dive
interview_reviews.quote: Very senior person. Discussed past work, why Adobe, challenges faced. Cultural fit assessment.

insider_tips: Adobe asks CS fundamentals — OS, DBMS, memory management are tested
C++ knowledge is highly valued — know pointers, virtual functions, templates
5-6 rounds is common — it's a thorough process
LLD is asked more than HLD for MTS-1
They value creative problem solvers — show unique approaches
Adobe has great WLB — mention you value that in HR rounds

do: Brush up on CS fundamentals (OS, DBMS)
dont: Skip OS/DBMS prep — it's a separate round
Think it's only DSA — Adobe tests breadth

impressive_projects.title: CRM Dashboard Builder
impressive_projects.desc: Drag-and-drop dashboard builder with custom widgets, data sources, and real-time metric updates.

impressive_projects.title: Workflow Automation Engine
impressive_projects.desc: Rule-based workflow engine with conditional triggers, email notifications, and approval chains.

impressive_projects.title: API Gateway & Rate Limiter
impressive_projects.desc: Build an API gateway with authentication, rate limiting, request logging, and multi-tenant support.

must_have: Enterprise SaaS or CRM related projects are strong signals

interview_reviews.question: 2-3 medium-hard DSA: binary tree diameter + matrix rotation
interview_reviews.quote: Standard OA with medium-hard DSA. Binary trees and matrix problems are common.

interview_reviews.question: LRU Cache design + implementation
interview_reviews.quote: Classic DS question. Implement from scratch including eviction. Discuss HashMap internals.

interview_reviews.question: 0-1 Knapsack variation + OOP discussion
interview_reviews.quote: DP problem with real-world context. Then discussed OOP principles and design patterns.

interview_reviews.question: Design Google Docs (collaborative editing)
interview_reviews.quote: HLD focus: architecture, scalability, SPOF handling. Very thorough discussion on trade-offs.

insider_tips: Salesforce has a unique 'Ohana' (family) culture — show you align with it
'Trust' is their #1 value — demonstrate integrity in behaviorals
Both LLD and HLD are tested — prepare both
HashMap internals are a favorite topic — know how they work
They may ask about Salesforce-specific tech (Apex, LWC) for CRM roles
Presentation round is sometimes included — be ready to present a technical topic

do: Align with Salesforce's trust and equality values
Be ready for a presentation round
dont: Forget to handle edge cases in coding
Be unprepared for system design at scale

impressive_projects.title: Employee Management Portal
impressive_projects.desc: CRUD app with role-based access, attendance tracking, leave management, and report generation.

impressive_projects.title: Student Database System
impressive_projects.desc: Academic record management with grade calculation, report cards, and data export.

must_have: Any full-stack project with proper documentation
Communication skills matter as much as coding at TCS

dsa.name: GeeksforGeeks DSA Course
dsa.desc: Best free DSA resource for placement prep

interview_reviews.question: Aptitude + basic coding (fibonacci, palindrome)
interview_reviews.quote: National Qualifier Test. Aptitude math + basic coding. Medium bar. Focus on completing all sections.

interview_reviews.question: DBMS queries + Java basics + project discussion
interview_reviews.quote: Basic technical round. SQL queries, Java OOP concepts, and project walk-through. Not difficult.

interview_reviews.question: Why TCS? Relocation? Bond discussion
interview_reviews.quote: Standard HR. Discuss 2-year bond, work location flexibility, and career goals.

insider_tips: TCS NQT is the main filter — clear it with good aptitude scores
Technical round is basic — Java/DBMS/OS fundamentals
Communication skills matter — show clarity and confidence
2-year service bond is standard — be prepared to discuss it
Focus on aptitude prep alongside coding
Mass recruiter — high acceptance rate compared to product companies

do: Show willingness to learn and relocate
dont: Be arrogant about your coding skills
Refuse to discuss the service bond

impressive_projects.title: Library Management System
impressive_projects.desc: Book catalog with member management, issue/return tracking, and fine calculation.

impressive_projects.title: Weather Dashboard App
impressive_projects.desc: Real-time weather data with location-based forecasts and historical trend charts.

interview_reviews.question: Aptitude + basic coding in Python/Java
interview_reviews.quote: InfyTQ certification exam. Easy coding, aptitude, and logical reasoning. Good score = direct interview.

interview_reviews.question: SQL joins + OOPS concepts + project discussion
interview_reviews.quote: Standard technical: OOP definitions, SQL queries, project walkthrough. Straightforward.

interview_reviews.question: Relocation flexibility? Gap year explanation?
interview_reviews.quote: Focus on flexibility, positive attitude, and career goals. Very friendly atmosphere.

insider_tips: InfyTQ certification gives you advantage in placement
Power Programmer role pays 2x — aim for it if you're strong in coding
Technical round is basic CS — OOP, DBMS, OS
Communication skills and attitude weigh heavily
Infosys values fresh graduates who show willingness to learn
SP (Specialist Programmer) track for higher CTC

do: Know OOP and SQL basics well

impressive_projects.title: Expense Tracker App
impressive_projects.desc: Personal finance tracker with categories, monthly reports, and budget alerts.

impressive_projects.title: Quiz Application
impressive_projects.desc: Online quiz platform with timer, scoring, leaderboard, and admin panel.

interview_reviews.question: Aptitude + verbal + basic coding
interview_reviews.quote: Standard aptitude test followed by 2 easy coding problems. Time management is key.

interview_reviews.question: Basic Java/Python + DBMS + project discussion
interview_reviews.quote: Asked about OOP pillars, SQL joins, and project walkthrough. Very basic.

interview_reviews.question: Salary expectations, relocation, strengths/weaknesses
interview_reviews.quote: Standard HR discussion. Be positive and show willingness to work anywhere.

insider_tips: Wipro Elite/NLTH programs offer higher packages
Aptitude + verbal is the main filter — practice mock tests
Technical round is basic programming concepts
Show eagerness to learn and adapt
Wipro values teamwork and flexibility
WILP (Work Integrated Learning Programme) is great for freshers
